detaileddescription.para: Value of the Internal oscillator in Hz
Freqency in kHz of the System tick
0 bit for pre-emption priority, 4 bits for subpriority
3 bits for pre-emption priority, 1 bit for subpriority
Scale 3 mode: the maximum value of fHCLK is 120 MHz.

Variable to store millisecond ticks.
It must be declared volatile! Just because it is modified in an ISR. Add it to the definition after static.

briefdescription.para: System Clock Configuration.

simplesect.para: This function should NOT be accesible from the outside to avoid configuration problems.
This function starts a system timer that generates a SysTick every 1 ms.

inbodydescription.para: Configure the main internal regulator output voltage

briefdescription.para: Setup the microcontroller system Initialize the FPU setting, vector table location and External memory configuration.

simplesect.para: This function is called at startup by CMSIS in startup_stm32f446xx.s.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the system.
Delays the program execution for the specified number of milliseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Number of milliseconds to delay.

briefdescription.para: Delays the program execution until the specified number of milliseconds since the system started.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the variable that stores the number of milliseconds to delay until.
Number of milliseconds to delay until.

simplesect.para: This function modifies the value of the variable pointed by t to the number of milliseconds to delay until.
This function is useful to implement periodic tasks.

briefdescription.para: Returns the number of milliseconds since the system started.

parameterdescription.para: of milliseconds since the system started.

briefdescription.para: Sets the number of milliseconds since the system started.

parameterdescription.para: New number of milliseconds since the system started.

memberdef.argsstring: (GPIO_TypeDef *p_port, uint8_t pin, uint8_t mode, uint8_t pupd)
memberdef.name: stm32f4_system_gpio_config

briefdescription.para: Configure the mode and pull of a GPIO.

para.verbatim:
============================================================================== ##### How to use GPIOs ##### ============================================================================== [..] (#) Enable the GPIO AHB clock using the RCC->AHB1ENR register.
(#) Configure the GPIO pin. (++) Configure the IO mode. (++) Activate Pull-up, Pull-down resistor. (++) In case of Output or alternate function mode, configure the speed if needed. (++) Configure digital or analog mode. (++) In case of external interrupt/event select the type (interrupt or event) and the corresponding trigger event (rising or falling or both).
(#) In case of external interrupt/event mode selection, configure NVIC IRQ priority mapped to the EXTI line and enable it using.
(#) To get the level of a pin configured in input mode use the GPIOx_IDR register.
(#) To set/reset the level of a pin configured in output mode use the GPIOx_BSRR register to SET (bits 0..15) or RESET (bits 16..31) the GPIO.

simplesect.para: This function performs the GPIO Port Clock Enable. It may occur that a port clock is re-enabled, it does not matter if it was already enabled. *

This function enables the AHB1 peripheral clock. After reset, the peripheral clock (used for registers read/write access) is disabled and the application software has to enable this clock before using it.

parameterdescription.para: Port of the GPIO (CMSIS struct like)
Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)
Input, output, alternate, or analog
Pull-up, pull-down, or no-pull

briefdescription.para: Configure the external interruption or event of a GPIO.

listitem.para: Enable the System configuration controller clock (SYSCFG). Enable the SYSCFG by setting the bit SYSCFGEN of the peripheral clock enable register (RCC_APB2ENR). The system configuration controller is used here to manage the external interrupt line connection to the GPIOs. 💡 As usual, you can access to the register (APB2ENR) as element of the structure RCC. You can use the macro RCC_APB2ENR_SYSCFGEN defined in stm32f446xx.h to set the bit. Look for the "RCC_APB2ENR" register in the Reference Manual if you need more information.

Associate the external interruption line to the given port. Clean and set the bits as shown in the tutorial document. 💡 Depending on the pin number, use the register SYSCFG_EXTICR1, SYSCFG_EXTICR2, SYSCFG_EXTICR3, or SYSCFG_EXTICR4. The structure SYSCFG contains a 4-element array called EXTICR; the first element (EXTICR[0]) configures the register SYSCFG_EXTICR1, and so on. 💡 To clean the EXTIx bits, you can create a mask depending on the pin value. 💡 To associate the external interruption to the given port, i.e. to set the EXTIx bits, you can create another mask depending on the port value.

Select the direction of the trigger: rising edge, falling edge, or both, depending on the value of the given mode. 💡 If rising edge: activate the corresponding bit on the EXTI_RTSR register (element RTSR) of the EXTI structure. 💡 If falling edge: activate the corresponding bit on the EXTI_FTSR register (element FTSR) of the EXTI structure. 💡 If both: activate the corresponding bit on both registers.

Select the interrupt and/or event request: depending on the value of the given mode. 💡 If event request enable: activate the corresponding bit on the EXTI_EMR register (element EMR) of the EXTI structure. 💡 If interrupt request enable: activate the corresponding bit on the EXTI_IMR register (element IMR) of the EXTI structure.

blockquote.para: 💡 You can define your own masks for each pin value (not recommended), or you can use the BIT_POS_TO_MASK(pin) macro to get the mask of a pin.

simplesect.para: It is highly recommended to clean the corresponding bit of each register (RSTR, FTSR, EMR, IMR) before activating it.

parameterdescription.para: Port of the GPIO (CMSIS struct like)
Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)
Trigger mode can be a combination (OR) of: (i) direction: rising edge (0x01), falling edge (0x02), (ii) event request (0x04), or (iii) interrupt request (0x08).

briefdescription.para: Enable interrupts of a GPIO line (pin).

parameterdescription.para: Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)
Priority level (from highest priority: 0, to lowest priority: 15)
Subpriority level (from highest priority: 0, to lowest priority: 15)

briefdescription.para: Disable interrupts of a GPIO line (pin)

parameterdescription.para: Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)

briefdescription.para: Read the digital value of a GPIO.

blockquote.para:
✅ 1. Retrieve the value of the IDR register. 💡 You must cast the read value to return a bool. 💡 You might use the BIT_POS_TO_MASK(pin) macro.
✅ 2. Return the value.

parameterdescription.para: Port of the GPIO (CMSIS struct like)
Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)

simplesect.para: true if the GPIO was HIGH
false if the GPIO was LOW

briefdescription.para: Write a digital value in a GPIO atomically.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Set the corresponding bit value of the BSRR register to set or reset the output depending on the given value. 💡 You might use the macros HIGH and LOW. 💡 You might use the BIT_POS_TO_MASK(pin) macro.

simplesect.para: You can use a +16 offset on the pin index and use the BIT_POS_TO_MASK(pin) macro to get the mask when you go to clear a GPIO. Otherwise, you can calculate the pin mask first and then use a 16-position left shift of the mask.

parameterdescription.para: Port of the GPIO (CMSIS struct like)
Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)
Boolean value to set the GPIO to HIGH (1, true) or LOW (0, false)

briefdescription.para: Toggle the value of a GPIO.

blockquote.para:
✅ 1. Read the value of the GPIO.
✅ 2. Write the opposite value in the GPIO. 💡 You might use functions stm32f4_system_gpio_read() and stm32f4_system_gpio_write() to help you. 💡 You might use the macros HIGH and LOW.

parameterdescription.para: Port of the GPIO (CMSIS struct like)
Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)

briefdescription.para: Configure the alternate function of a GPIO.

listitem.para: Shift left the mask depending on the value of the given **pin modulo 8.** 💡 The value of pin ranges from 0 to 15. The registers GPIOx_AFRH and GPIOx_AFRL implement 8 groups of 4 bits each. In order to use the value of pin as index to select the corresponding group of bits, we can use the remainder of the division by 8.

Clean and set the bits as shown in the tutorial document. 💡 Clean the corresponding bit on element 0 or 1 of the AFR array (e.g, GPIOA->AFR[0] for GPIOx_AFRL) 💡 Set the given value (alternate) of the alternate function, using bit shifting, for example.

blockquote.para: 💡 You can define your own masks for each alternate function (not recommended), or you can use the macro BASE_MASK_TO_POS(m, p) to get the mask of a base mask. Example: A base mask m equals 0x03 (0b 0000 0011 in binary) can be shifted p equals 8 positions BASE_MASK_TO_POS(0x03, 8) resulting in 0x300 (0b 0011 0000 0000 in binary).

simplesect.para: The AFR register is a 2-element array representing GPIO alternate function high an low registers (GPIOx_AFRH and GPIOx_AFRL) AFRLy: Alternate function selection for port x pin y (y = 0..7) AFRHy: Alternate function selection for port x pin y (y = 8..15)

parameterdescription.para: Port of the GPIO (CMSIS struct like)
Pin/line of the GPIO (index from 0 to 15)
Alternate function number (values from 0 to 15) according to table of the datasheet: "Table 11. Alternate function".

briefdescription.para: Resume Tick increment.

detaileddescription.para: The SysTick timer is the source of time base. It is used to generate interrupts at regular time intervals. Once this function is called, the SysTick interrupt will be enabled and so Tick increment is resumed.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Copy the following code in the file stm32f4_system.c.

briefdescription.para: Suspend Tick increment.

detaileddescription.para: The SysTick timer is the source of time base. It is used to generate interrupts at regular time intervals. Once this function is called, the SysTick interrupt will be disabled so it saves more energy and it does not generate any interruption.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Copy the following code in the file stm32f4_system.c.

briefdescription.para: Set the system in stop mode for low power consumption.

detaileddescription.para: The function sets the state of the power regulator in stop mode. After that, the function sets the system mode in sleep mode, to enter in stop mode when calling __WFI() (wait for interruption). The system remains in this line until an external interruption or timer interruption occurs. If an interruption occurs the system wakes up and resets the system mode.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Copy the following code in the file stm32f4_system.c.

briefdescription.para: Set the system in sleep mode for low power consumption.

detaileddescription.para:
The function sets the state of the power regulator in sleep mode. After that, the function sets the system mode in sleep mode, to enter in sleep mode when calling __WFI() (wait for interruption). The system remains in this line until an external interruption or timer interruption occurs.
ONLY IF YOU NEED IT! TODO alumnos Version 4:
✅ 1. Copy the following code in the file stm32f4_system.c.

briefdescription.para: Enable low power consumption in sleep mode.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_system_systick_suspend() to suspend the count of the SysTick. ✅ 2. Call function port_system_power_sleep() to enter sleep in sleep mode.

briefdescription.para: This file implements port layer for the system functions in the STM32F4 platform.
